title: Handle Keyboard and Mouse Actions
description: Learn to simulate keyboard inputs and mouse actions in Playwright
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Uses keyboard.press for key actions
2. Performs Ctrl+A and other shortcuts
3. Hovers over elements
4. Double-clicks elements
5. Right-clicks for context menu

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_keyboard_mouse(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    # TODO: Type using keyboard
    
    # TODO: Use keyboard shortcuts
    
    # TODO: Hover over element
    
    # TODO: Double-click if applicable
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_keyboard_mouse(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://demo.playwright.dev/todomvc')
    
    input_field = page.get_by_placeholder('What needs to be done?')
    input_field.click()
    
    # Type and press Enter
    input_field.type('First Task')
    page.keyboard.press('Enter')
    
    # Add another task and select all
    input_field.type('Second Task')
    page.keyboard.press('Control+A')
    page.keyboard.press('Backspace')
    
    # Type new task
    input_field.type('New Task')
    input_field.press('Enter')
    
    # Hover over todo item
    todo = page.locator('.todo-list li').first
    todo.hover()
    
    # Verify delete button becomes visible on hover
    expect(todo.locator('.destroy')).to_be_visible()
    
    # Double-click to edit
    label = todo.locator('label')
    label.dblclick()

hints: Use .type() for typing text character by character
Use .hover() to move mouse over element
